response.original_title: Dorchester Academy: A Legacy of Liberty
response.overview: A documentary film highlighting a former African-American boarding school in South Georgia that went on to play a pivotal role in the Civil Rights Movement. Ambassador Andrew Young joins local historians in recalling how he, Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr., and other Civil Rights icons came to Liberty County in the 1960s to teach citizenship and voting rights to hundreds of Black citizens who traveled to the idyllic campus. This documentary reveals not only seldom-told local history, but significant American history. This film is dedicated to promoting, protecting, and preserving the treasured legacy of a place, a people, and a history that changed the world.
response.title: Dorchester Academy: A Legacy of Liberty
